headline: Sandisk and Micron sink as rotation trade builds
detail: Money rotating out of semis is hitting memory names hard today, and that same rotation pressure is the direct reason SMH and SOXL are struggling in your book. Supply shortages may limit how far they fall, but rotation can persist for weeks regardless of fundamentals. Your leveraged SOXL position amplifies every down day in this environment.
rec: Strongly consider trimming or stopping out SOXL near current levels - the rotation trend plus 3x leverage plus your existing -14.5% loss is a dangerous combo on margin. No scanner long setup supports adding here.

headline: ServiceNow and Salesforce look like buys, Armageddon fears too extreme
detail: The analyst call on NOW and CRM suggests enterprise software is oversold, which is adjacent to your tech concentration but not a direct holding. Your scanner also shows NOW (8/12) with a watch entry at 105.59-105.69, stop 104.82. The headline gives a fundamental reason to pay attention to that setup.
rec: NOW has both an analyst catalyst and a scanner setup (entry 105.59-105.69, stop 104.82) - worth watching for a clean entry, but it's grade B and daily trend is down, so only consider a small starter position, not a full swing on margin.

headline: This unheralded sector may be the one sure thing for bulls in July
detail: Combined with headline 1 on space and defense, this points to rotation away from your tech-heavy names toward sectors you don't hold. It doesn't give you a direct trade but confirms the headwind your book is facing this month.
rec: No action warranted - you have no defense or space exposure and this isn't your game. The value here is as context: rotation is real and your semi positions are feeling it.

headline: Is Meta giving up on cutting-edge AI? Wall Street divided over cloud pivot
detail: Uncertainty around Meta's AI direction matters for the broader AI/tech sentiment that props up names like CRWD and the QQQ. A divided Wall Street on AI leadership is a mild negative for high-multiple tech names in your book. Not a crisis, but worth monitoring if the narrative shifts further.
rec: No trade needed today, but if AI sentiment sours materially, CRWD at +7.4% is the position most exposed to a narrative-driven selloff - keep that mental stop tight.
